scenario_name: K-12 tutoring assistant with student-progress context
description: A school district deploys an AI tutoring assistant for K-12 students. The assistant retrieves over curriculum content, references the student's prior-assignment performance, and tailors explanations. The deployment processes minors' education records (FERPA-protected, COPPA-relevant for under-13s) and faces unique obligations around content appropriateness and parental consent.
assumptions: Hosted LLM under enterprise educational data-isolation contract.
Student records are restricted by district policy; parents have access rights.
Content filtering is age-appropriate per grade level.

components.description: Student information system holding records, assignments, and grades.
Curriculum-content retrieval scoped to grade level.
District SSO with role attributes (student / teacher / parent).
Audit log retained per district records retention; FERPA-aligned.

risks_identified.rationale: Student records (PII of minors, learning-disability accommodations, behavioral notes) are highly sensitive.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-student scope at retrieval; output PII redaction.

risks_identified.rationale: FERPA, COPPA (under-13), state student-privacy laws, possible EU AI Act high-risk classification for educational scoring.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Parental consent captured; AI register entry pending; FERPA disclosures aligned with district policy.

risks_identified.rationale: Student-to-student context bleed would be a district-wide privacy incident.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-student retrieval filter; no cross-student memory store.

risks_identified.rationale: Children may attempt manipulation to bypass content filters; injection from external homework-help content surfaces is plausible.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Age-appropriate classifier; refusal stability tested across known jailbreak corpora.

risks_identified.rationale: Teacher annotation of tutoring quality exposes annotators to student data beyond their classroom.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Annotation scoped to teacher's own students.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs hold student-progress data subject to FERPA disclosure to parents.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: FERPA-aligned access; parent-portal integrated for record requests.

existing_controls.description: Per-student retrieval filter; no cross-student memory.
Age-appropriate-content classifier on every output by grade.
Parental consent captured pre-enrollment; opt-out path supported.
FERPA-aligned audit log access; parent-portal integration for record requests.
Annotation scoped to teacher's own students.

control_gaps.description: EU AI Act classification for educational scoring is unresolved (may be high-risk).
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Complete classification analysis; document conformity assessment if high-risk.

control_gaps.description: Refusal stability is tested at release but not continuously.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add continuous refusal-rate monitoring against an evolving jailbreak corpus.

control_gaps.description: No formal periodic notice to parents about AI use in instruction.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Annual AI-use notice integrated with district communication cycles.
